key_themes: Q4 earnings beat with 2026 margin recovery and growth guidance
Cautious consumer spending pressures on retail
summary: Target (TGT) sentiment is solidly positive as the stock has rallied over 30% YTD, hit 52-week highs near $128, and broken out of a multi-year downtrend amid a Q4 2025 earnings beat ($2.44 EPS vs ~$2.17 expected) and constructive 2026 guidance for ~2% sales growth plus margin expansion.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/target-beats-q4-earnings-estimates-145700459.html)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TGT/) Analysts including Jefferies, Morgan Stanley, and DA Davidson have turned more bullish on the recovery, citing accelerating inventory turns, exclusive collections, and wellness initiatives. While X chatter highlights broader consumer weakness (rising searches for budget meals), investor focus remains on undervaluation versus peers, technical strength, and upcoming profit growth.
sources: 20+ news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, CNBC, Jefferies, Morgan Stanley, company releases), 18 recent X posts
